evidence: In December 2024, Visa acquired Featurespace Limited, a developer of real-time artificial intelligence payments protection technology...for a purchase consideration of $946 million.
Visa continues to invest in the development and deployment of next-generation technologies, such as generative AI (GenAI), stablecoins and agentic commerce.
Visa s commitment to innovation and collaboration is reflected in how we equip employees with GenAI capabilities...our internal GenAI hub...Visa s own AI assistant for employees.
From its launch in March 2025 through the fiscal year ended September 30, 2025, it has been embraced by nearly 26,000 employees launching over 261,000 AI-powered chats.
Visa Consumer Authentication Service provides an AI-powered, real-time, network-agnostic risk score that enables clients to reduce fraud and false declines.
In 2025, we deployed a pilot program that processed live agentic token transactions within the ecommerce and enterprise environments.

The adoption of agentic commerce...presents novel and complex regulatory, privacy and cybersecurity risks...may see increased instances of erroneous or disputed payments, increased chargebacks and reputational harm.
Risks include...intellectual property considerations, the collection and use of personal data, third party risks, technical limitations of algorithms and the accuracy of training data.

The EU has adopted a comprehensive AI Act...with key provisions for high-risk AI systems taking effect in August 2026...may limit the ability to develop and deploy AI systems or significantly increase associated compliance costs.

capex_mentions.quote: acquired Featurespace Limited...for a purchase consideration of $946 million
capex_mentions.context: Fiscal 2025 acquisition of AI-powered payments fraud protection technology company, December 2024

limitations: No multi-year AI-specific capex or R&D dollar totals disclosed beyond the Featurespace acquisition; investment intensity score of 4 reflects a single quantified AI-attributable transaction plus named but unquantified ongoing programs.

Narrative concreteness score of 5 is supported by employee adoption metrics (26,000 users, 261,000 chats) and a live agentic pilot; some product claims remain promotional without external outcome metrics (e.g., fraud reduction rates).

Risk disclosure excerpts span both [business] and [risk_factors] sections and contain detailed AI-specific risk mechanisms, supporting a score of 4; no quantified risk exposure or named governance body was identified to warrant a score of 5.
